policy.title: RBI Finalizes Climate Risk Disclosure Guidelines for Scheduled Commercial Banks
policy.description: RBI released final guidelines mandating SCBs to integrate climate scenario analysis into ICAAP by March 2027, with quantitative PD/LGD stress testing required for top-100 corporate exposures. Banks must report Scope 1-2 financed emissions and physical risk exposure by geography starting FY27.

policy.title: SEBI Expands BRSR Core to Top 500 Listed Companies
policy.description: SEBI extended mandatory BRSR Core reporting to top 500 companies by market cap effective FY27, adding water stress metrics and supply chain Scope 3 emissions. Third-party assurance now required for 9 KPIs including GHG intensity and energy consumption.
policy.source: Securities and Exchange Board of India

policy.title: IRDAI Issues Guidelines on Index-Based Parametric Crop and Property Products
policy.description: IRDAI published a regulatory sandbox framework for parametric insurance products tied to rainfall, temperature, and wind speed indices. Insurers can now partner with banks for bundled agri-loan products with automatic payouts triggered at pre-defined thresholds.
policy.source: Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority of India

policy.title: Basel Committee Finalizes Pillar 3 Climate Risk Disclosure Framework
policy.description: BCBS released final Pillar 3 disclosure requirements for banks' climate-related financial risks, with phased implementation starting January 2027. Framework mandates disclosure of physical and transition risk exposures by sector and geography, with standardized metrics for credit risk concentration.

policy.title: ISSB Releases Guidance on Emerging Market Application of IFRS S2
policy.description: ISSB published implementation guidance specifically addressing proportionality for emerging market jurisdictions, including simplified Scope 3 methodologies and transitional reliefs for scenario analysis. India's MCA signaled intention to adopt modified IFRS S1/S2 standards by 2028.

policy.title: NGFS Releases Phase V Climate Scenarios with Enhanced Physical Risk Pathways
policy.description: NGFS updated its climate scenarios to include acute physical risk tipping points and regional granularity for South Asia. New scenarios model monsoon variability impacts on agricultural credit portfolios with 10km resolution data for India.
policy.source: Network for Greening the Financial System

competitors.title: Jupiter Intelligence Partners with HDFC Bank for Physical Risk Assessment
competitors.description: Jupiter announced a multi-year partnership with HDFC Bank to provide asset-level physical risk scoring for the bank's commercial real estate and infrastructure portfolio. Deal reportedly covers 50,000+ assets across flood, cyclone, and heat stress perils.

competitors.title: MSCI Launches India Climate Value-at-Risk Module for Banks
competitors.description: MSCI expanded its Climate VaR product with an India-specific module covering 800+ listed companies with physical and transition risk scores aligned to RBI's new climate disclosure framework. Product integrates with banks' existing credit risk systems.

competitors.title: Sprih Raises $12M Series A for ESG Compliance Platform
competitors.description: Bangalore-based Sprih closed a $12M Series A led by Accel to expand its BRSR reporting automation platform. Company claims 150+ enterprise clients and plans to add climate risk analytics for NBFC customers in Q3 2026.

competitors.title: XDI Releases India Physical Risk Database with Taluk-Level Resolution
competitors.description: Australian climate analytics firm XDI launched a granular physical risk database covering all Indian taluks with compound risk scores for flood, heat, and cyclone. Database marketed to Indian banks for RBI climate stress testing compliance.

competitors.title: Moody's Partners with CRISIL for India Climate-Adjusted Credit Ratings
competitors.description: Moody's and CRISIL announced a joint methodology for integrating physical climate risk into credit ratings for Indian infrastructure and real estate issuers. Pilot covers 200 rated entities with expected full rollout by September 2026.

competitors.title: CarbonMint Launches Parametric Insurance Platform with Kotak General
competitors.description: CarbonMint partnered with Kotak General Insurance to launch a blockchain-based parametric insurance product for smallholder farmers in Maharashtra. Platform uses satellite-derived rainfall indices for automatic claim settlement within 72 hours.

research.title: Climate Risk and Financial Stability in South Asia: A Supervisory Perspective
research.description: BIS study finds South Asian banks face 12-18% potential NPL increase under 2°C warming scenario, with agricultural and MSME portfolios most vulnerable. Report recommends supervisors mandate forward-looking climate stress tests with minimum 30-year horizons.

research.title: Closing the Protection Gap: Parametric Solutions for Climate Perils in India
research.description: Swiss Re estimates India's climate protection gap at $45B annually, with parametric products capable of addressing 30-40% of uninsured agricultural and SME losses. Report identifies monsoon variability and urban flood as highest-potential product categories.

research.title: ESG Data Availability in Emerging Markets: 2026 Assessment
research.description: IFC analysis reveals only 23% of Indian SMEs have any climate-related disclosures, creating significant data gaps for lender assessments. Report calls for alternative data approaches including supply chain proxies and satellite-derived environmental metrics.

research.title: Physical Climate Risk Assessment for Indian Banking Sector
research.description: CRISIL analysis of 35 banks finds Rs 8.2 lakh crore in credit exposure concentrated in high physical risk districts, with coastal and flood-prone regions accounting for 22% of aggregate advances. Study recommends risk-based pricing adjustments of 25-75 bps for high-risk assets.

research.title: The Economics of Index-Based Insurance in Climate Adaptation Finance
research.description: World Bank finds parametric insurance can reduce post-disaster fiscal burden by 40-60% when bundled with development lending in climate-vulnerable regions. India case study shows 3:1 benefit-cost ratio for monsoon-indexed products in rainfed agriculture zones.

research.title: Integrating Climate Risk into Credit Risk Frameworks: Practical Approaches
research.description: UNEP FI pilot with 12 emerging market banks demonstrates methodology for translating physical hazard data into PD adjustments, finding 50-200 bps impact on agricultural loan pricing under moderate warming scenarios. Framework validated for Indian bank portfolios.

ourRead.headline: RBI's final climate guidelines create immediate demand for physical risk PD models—but global competitors are already knocking on Indian bank doors.
body: The signals from the past 60 days are unambiguous: Indian financial regulators have moved from consultation to mandate, and the compliance clock is now ticking. RBI's finalized climate disclosure guidelines, SEBI's BRSR Core expansion, and IRDAI's parametric sandbox collectively create the regulatory infrastructure StepChange has been anticipating. The question is no longer whether Indian FIs will need climate risk intelligence—it's who will provide it.

The RBI's March 28 final guidelines are the headline event. The requirement for quantitative PD/LGD stress testing on top-100 corporate exposures by March 2027 is precisely StepChange's sweet spot. However, the specification of scenario analysis aligned with NGFS Phase V scenarios (which now include India-specific monsoon variability) means our models must incorporate these pathways immediately. The Basel Pillar 3 finalization adds global pressure—Indian banks with international operations face dual compliance burdens that favor integrated solutions.

The competitor signals demand urgent attention. Jupiter's HDFC partnership and XDI's taluk-level India database represent direct competitive threats to our climate risk bet. MSCI's India Climate VaR module, explicitly aligned to RBI requirements, shows the global players are adapting fast. Most concerning: Moody's-CRISIL partnership embeds climate risk into credit ratings—a move toward the analytical layer we occupy. On parametric, CarbonMint's Kotak deal validates the market but captures first-mover positioning we should have claimed. Sprih's fundraise suggests ESG compliance software is commoditizing; our differentiation must remain in analytical depth, not reporting automation.

The BIS and CRISIL research provide immediate ammunition. CRISIL's Rs 8.2 lakh crore high-risk exposure figure is a compelling sales datapoint—we should build district-level overlays against bank branch networks to quantify this for specific institutions. The UNEP FI pilot demonstrating 50-200 bps PD impacts validates our pricing thesis; we need case studies showing similar granularity. IFC's 23% disclosure figure for Indian SMEs underscores the alternative data opportunity in our Global South bet—satellite and supply chain proxies are not optional, they're essential.

Watch: IRDAI's parametric sandbox applications—first-mover insurers will set product standards. Action: Fast-track an RBI compliance readiness assessment for three mid-tier banks before Jupiter and XDI lock up the top-10.
